transcript.clean_text: foreign hosted the final stage of the 106th Judd Italia in all its Glory with the Peloton ready to taste one final bit of Glory the Jersey's all ready to be awarded the GC more or less done and those who wanted to go for a final stage win most likely the sprinters were all ready for action signing in meant signing out the 21st and final stage of the race just hours after disappointment for some glory for others and a dramatic change in the GC at the top of a mountain in the far Northeast of the country they spent their morning on a plane they'd spend their afternoon on the bike riding out to the Sea back into town and six laps around the center of Rome the Eternal City the Italian Capital with all the monuments of ancient Rome on show 126 kilometers in the end for the 21st and final stage of the jeweler the day began with the usual sipping of bubbles photographs and the pink jersey on show Primos Shabbat to win the general Italia by 14 seconds Nicolas delavale would finish last but well over five hours down they rolled out to the seaside but a day before their holidays could begin they would ride back into the Italian capital and the attacks would begin to try and win the stage with just under 70 kilometers to go Benedetti squish with the three attackers they got a maximum gap of almost 40 seconds the bit by bit they'd be pulled back in by the chasing sprinters teams at the intermediate Sprints coins would do his best to try and stop Derek G winning the intermediate Sprints competition a task he pulled off successfully the Sprint teams continued their work and going into the final lap the Breakaway would be caught one lap to go just over 13 kilometers and the cobbles outside the Coliseum were awaiting final mechanical problems for Meyer offer and Dunbar were successfully negotiated so too a lead out for Mark Cavendish even Garrett Thomas was starting to help out he pulled things along and once they got into the final few hundred meters it would be Pascual long trying to lead up Milan gavilia going from distance Cavendish the British champion on his final ever gilded Italia stage coming off the wheel of the Colombian hitting the front staying at the front and in the Eternal City the Eternal Sprinter the greatest of all time Mark Cavendish winning with a crash behind taking out Pascal Ackerman Seven Trips to the Judo a stage at each and every one of those a 17th julo stage in the end disappointment for others but all in all everybody finishing and Mark Cavendish Victorious again at a grand tour his first victory of the Season since joining Astana Kazakhstan who pulled off their first win for four years at this race a bit of help from his friends finished off however by nobody else apart from himself the max missile firing for one final time to Victory and Glory a bucket list Sprint in Rome won by Mark Cavendish head of Alex Kish Felipe fiorelli with Dainese and Krieger in the top five all that suffering in the mountains worthwhile for one final shot at glory all the glory on the final Podium belonged to Primos roglich in the pink jersey and becoming the first ever Slovenian to win the Judd Italia he beats garan Thomas by 14 seconds and Joel maida becoming the first Portuguese ever to finish on a Giro podium the never-ending trophy has a new name on it a new nationality too as jumbovismus Primo roglich wins his fourth grand tour at his first Judo white jersey went to join while made up the Maya ciclamino the overall point classification Was Won by an Italian Jonathan Milan add in his final jittery Talia tibopino won the king of the mountains competition as well as taking a top five overall in the GC the best team were Bahrain Victorious and ajiro ending in front of a place where the great Gladiators once thought it out the modern day equivalent treating us to a final week of big entertainment from the jittery Talia thanks for watching on gcn Plus
